topic: Viral recruiter teardown score receipt clips
thesis: Turn a timely job-search pain point into one useful long-form teardown and several short proof clips.

sourceNotes.title: YouTube Partner Program eligibility
sourceNotes.note: Use YouTube as a monetization target, but optimize first for site conversions.

sourceNotes.title: Pew Research Center Social Media Fact Sheet
sourceNotes.note: YouTube, Instagram, and TikTok are large discovery channels for job-search education.

episodeScript.title: Resume Roast: The Job Seeker's Guide to Aces and Fails

chapters.title: Introduction
chapters.content: Open with the line: 'Ever feel like your resume is a sad, former version of yourself? Like a Scooby-Doo villain?'

chapters.title: The Resume Crime Scene
chapters.content: Show a resume with vague phrases. Roast phrases like 'team player' and turn them into ridiculous job descriptions.

chapters.title: ATS Myth Lab
chapters.content: Confusion about applicant tracking systems – pull in visuals of common myths and the confusion they create.

chapters.title: One Bullet Fix
chapters.content: Take one bad resume bullet and show how to replace it with two strong ones; visualize the transformation with comedic flair.

chapters.title: Final Thoughts and Call to Action
chapters.content: Wrap it up with 'Want your Signal score? Test it now for free and elevate that sad resume into a glorious rock star!'

youtube.title: Viral recruiter teardown score receipt clips | Resume Teardown
youtube.seoTitle: Viral recruiter teardown score receipt clips | Resume Teardown | Signal by ATSHacker
youtube.description: A funny recruiter-style teardown of why a qualified resume can be hard to find, how to translate a job description into resume proof, and where Signal fits without fake claims.
youtube.cta: Paste the job description and check your free Signal score before you apply.

scriptSections.label: Cold open
scriptSections.script: Viral recruiter teardown score receipt clips This resume looks professional, which is why the mistake is dangerous. On screen, it feels polished. In a recruiter search, the proof is still hard to find.
scriptSections.visual: Cold open with comic red stamp, quick zoom, Signal mascot reaction, and resume/JD split screen.

scriptSections.label: Open loop
scriptSections.script: We are not adding fake experience. We are checking whether the resume explains the real experience in the same language as the target job.
scriptSections.visual: Show the weak resume score, then hold the improved score as the unresolved payoff.

scriptSections.label: Translate the job description
scriptSections.script: Before touching the resume, pull out the role title, tools, responsibilities, metrics, and proof signals the job actually asks for.
scriptSections.visual: Resume on left, job description on right, mismatched terms highlighted with funny labels.

scriptSections.label: Recruiter search test
scriptSections.script: If a recruiter searches for the first three important terms from the job description, this resume should produce useful hits. Right now, the source bullet is too broad.
scriptSections.visual: Search console typing the first three job-description terms and showing weak matches.

scriptSections.label: Resume crime scene
scriptSections.script: Now compare the resume line by line. The weak bullet is not dishonest. It is just too vague to carry the role-specific proof.

scriptSections.label: Why it gets missed
scriptSections.script: A polished sentence can still hide the most important evidence. Recruiters skim for tools, scope, and outcomes because those make fit easy to verify.
scriptSections.visual: Zoom into the weak bullet and place missing proof labels beside it.

scriptSections.label: Live bullet fix
scriptSections.script: The fix is to say the real work clearly: what tool, what scope, what audience, and what result. Same person, clearer evidence.

scriptSections.label: Score reveal
scriptSections.script: Once the evidence matches the role language, the example score moves up. The point is not gaming a robot. The point is making real fit easier to see.
scriptSections.visual: Score meter jumps from low match to high match with before/after resume panels.

scriptSections.label: Trust and CTA
scriptSections.script: Signal automates this comparison. Paste the job description, upload the resume, and check the free score first. If the gap is real, then the paid resume and cover letter package makes sense.
scriptSections.visual: Free Signal score page, trust badges, and bundle CTA.

shorts.title: This resume sentence is quietly expensive
shorts.script: Stop on this line. Job asks HubSpot workflows, LinkedIn Ads, and CAC analysis. Resume says: Helped maintain HubSpot lists for webinars and nurture emails. Starts at 34 because one job term appears, no metric, hidden outcome. Rewrite the real work: built lead scoring and retargeting that cut CAC 32 percent. Receipt fills: terms, tool, result. Score lands at 92. Run the free Signal score before you apply.
storyboard: Case: Marketing Coordinator -> Demand Gen Candidate against Demand Generation Manager.
Cold open: show the weak bullet with an INTERVIEW TAX stamp.
Cut to a three-item score receipt: HubSpot workflows, LinkedIn Ads, CAC analysis.
Show why the starting score is low before the rewrite.
Signal drags the real proof into a receipt-style rewrite.
Reveal the improved score only after the checklist fills.
End with a free Signal score CTA, not a hard product pitch.

signalLines.teardown: Roast the bullet, rescue the person.

Helped maintain HubSpot lists for webinars and nurture emails.

props.afterBullet: Built HubSpot lead-scoring and LinkedIn retargeting segments that cut CAC 32% and lifted MQL-to-SQL conversion 18%.

jobDescription.title: Demand Generation Manager
jobDescription.summary: TargetCo is hiring a Demand Generation Manager who can connect practical execution with measurable business outcomes.
responsibilities: Use HubSpot workflows and LinkedIn Ads to improve team execution and reporting.
Translate work into measurable outcomes tied to CAC analysis.
Partner cross-functionally and communicate progress clearly to stakeholders.

shorts.title: I searched Salesforce and this resume vanished
shorts.script: Recruiter search test. I search Salesforce. The search box ghosted it. I try pipeline generation; still nothing useful. Resume says: Responsible for sales opportunities in assigned territory. Starts at 37 because: missing tool, missing pipeline proof. Rewrite it: generated 1.8 million in qualified Salesforce pipeline. Now Salesforce has a receipt. Score lands at 88. Run the free Signal score before you send yours.
storyboard: Case: Account Executive Resume against Mid-Market Account Executive.
Open with a big search box typing Salesforce; show the miss immediately.
Rapid-cut pipeline generation and show the second miss.
Reveal the resume bullet as the reason the search failed.
Show a score receipt explaining the low start.
Rewrite the bullet and rerun the search with a clear match.
Close with the score jump and free score CTA.

shorts.title: This resume failed an open-book test
shorts.script: Job post gave the answer key: React, TypeScript, Next.js. Resume says: Worked on frontend features for internal and customer-facing tools. Starts at 41 because the stack and metric are missing, and the result is hidden. Rewrite with real proof: shipped React and TypeScript checkout work that reduced drop-off 14 percent. Now keywords and outcome match. Score reaches 90. Run the free Signal score before you apply.
storyboard: Case: Frontend Engineer Resume against Frontend Software Engineer.
Open on an answer-key board: React, TypeScript, Next.js.
Slide the resume beside it and show the weak mismatch immediately.
Show a score receipt: missing stack, missing metric, missing result.
Signal translates hidden proof into job-description language.
Reveal the rewritten bullet, then fill the score checklist.
End with the score jump and free Signal score CTA.

monetization.strategy: YouTube monetization through ads, optimized for site conversions via the free Signal score offer. Drive traffic from video to landing page.
monetization.callToAction: Promote the free Signal score throughout, leading viewers to consider premium offerings after they've tasted success.
monetization.primaryGoal: Site revenue from resume / cover letter packages, not ad revenue.
monetization.cta: Free Signal score -> $9.99 resume or $14.99 bundle.
monetization.upsell: Promote bundle after score completion and multi-role pack after first purchase.

viewerCustomerReview.insight: Viewers love a mix of humor and helpful tips; resonates better when the pain points of job searching are addressed without mocking them.

notes: Needs more safe humor that punches at vague resume language or job-search friction.
